pki.ct.parseLogList.reason: The RFC 6962 CT log-list trust surface ingests the log_list.json into constraint-carrying trusted logs (each log id recomputed as SHA-256 of its key, with state and temporal-interval gates), verified against the live browser log list, but the live-fetch client for the list is still Planned and the trust model continues to absorb refinements. Graduate once the log-list client surface is settled.

pki.ct.verifySctWithLogList.reason: Resolves the log key from an SCT's log id against the pki.ct.parseLogList trusted set and enforces the log state and temporal-interval gates before delegating the signature check to verifySct; rides the same still-settling log-list trust surface. Graduate alongside pki.ct.parseLogList.

pki.ct.verifyLogListSignature.reason: Verifies the detached log_list.sig over the raw log-list bytes against a caller-pinned signer key, cross-checked against openssl dgst -verify, but the surrounding log-list trust surface (parse plus a live-fetch client) is still settling. Graduate alongside pki.ct.parseLogList.

pki.schema.c509.parse.reason: C509 (draft-ietf-cose-cbor-encoded-cert) is a moving Internet-Draft, not a published standard; the parser covers both certificate modes with byte-exact type-3 DER reconstruction and is proven against derived known-answer vectors, but the wire format can still change before the draft is finalized. Graduate once the C509 draft is published as an RFC.

pki.schema.c509.encode.reason: The C509 producing side (a DER X.509 certificate compressed to a byte-exact-invertible type-3 C509 over the deterministic-CBOR encoder) rides the same draft-ietf-cose-cbor-encoded-cert wire format as pki.schema.c509.parse, which can still change before the draft is finalized. Graduate alongside pki.schema.c509.parse once the C509 draft is published as an RFC.

pki.smime.sign.reason: The RFC 8551 S/MIME message layer (multipart/signed clear-signed + application/pkcs7-mime opaque) is hardened and openssl-interop-proven, but the surrounding S/MIME surface is still growing (encryption, compression, capability negotiation) and the canonicalization/CTE handling continues to absorb interop refinements. Graduate once the full S/MIME message surface is settled.

pki.smime.verify.reason: Rides the same lib/mime.js canonicalizer and content-binding path as pki.smime.sign; graduate alongside it once the S/MIME message surface is settled.

pki.ct.encodeSctList.reason: The RFC 6962 SCT producing surface (the TLS-encoded SignedCertificateTimestampList) is complete and round-trips through the shipped pki.ct.parseSctList, but it is not yet cross-checked against an independent CT log's own encoding in the integration harness. Graduate once an external CT toolchain validates a list it emits.

pki.ct.signSct.reason: The RFC 6962 SCT signing surface rides the same producing-side path as pki.ct.encodeSctList and is not yet interop-proven against an independent CT verifier. Graduate alongside pki.ct.encodeSctList.

pki.webauthn.parseAttestationObject.reason: The attestation-object surface is hardened and covers the WebAuthn attestation-statement formats, but the format set is still growing and the aaguid->root binding via FIDO MDS is the caller's step rather than an in-toolkit surface. Graduate once the attestation-format coverage and the metadata-service trust-anchor binding are settled.

pki.webauthn.verify.reason: The attestation verifier's surface (opts, the returned trust path) tracks the still-growing attestation-format set and defers the trust-path-to-anchor chaining to the caller (pki.path.validate + FIDO MDS). Graduate alongside pki.webauthn.parseAttestationObject.

pki.path.validate.reason: The RFC 5280 Â§6 path-validation surface is broad and still hardening, and it is not yet interop-proven against an independent path validator in the integration harness. Graduate once the verify surface has stabilized and an external validator cross-checks a path it accepts/rejects.

pki.path.crlChecker.reason: Rides the same SIG_ALGS verify bridge as pki.path.validate and the same not-yet-interop-proven revocation path. Graduate alongside pki.path.validate.

pki.schema.engine.encode.reason: The schema engine's encode direction backs the round-trip tests but has no external consumer that pins its API; the combinator surface is still absorbing new leaves. Graduate once the combinator set is settled and an operator-facing encode path depends on it.

pki.schema.engine.embeddedDer.reason: The embedded-DER leaf is part of the still-settling engine combinator surface; it has no external consumer pinning its return shape. Graduate alongside pki.schema.engine.encode once the leaf set is settled.

pki.ct.parseSctList.reason: The RFC 6962 signed-certificate-timestamp list parser is not yet interop-proven end-to-end: no test in the harness verifies an SCT it decodes against a production CT log's public key, so its return shape is not frozen. Graduate once an independent verifier cross-checks an SCT it parses.

pki.ct.reconstructSignedData.reason: Reconstructs the RFC 6962 digitally-signed structure that an SCT signature covers; it is unverified against an independent CT verifier end-to-end, the same interop gap as pki.ct.parseSctList. Graduate alongside pki.ct.parseSctList.

pki.ct.verifySct.reason: RFC 6962 sec. 3.2 SCT verification is settled in standard but not cross-implementation-interop-proven: the harness proves it via a self round-trip (real node crypto signs, verifySct verifies) and against real certificate SCT extensions, but no offline oracle signs an SCT for an independent cross-check, so the boolean/opts surface is not frozen. Graduate once the SCT-list encode/sign producing side and a production-CT-log SCT cross-check land.

pki.schema.smime.parseSigningCertificate.reason: The RFC 5035 / RFC 8551 S/MIME ESS SigningCertificate decoder is not yet interop-proven: the openssl-cms oracle for the ESS signing-certificate attribute is available but no fixtures row is registered in the integration harness yet (auto-interop.test.js flags exactly this), so no independent implementation round-trips a structure it decodes. Graduate once an interop fixtures row exercises an ESS attribute through OpenSSL.

The RFC 5035 ESS SigningCertificateV2 decoder rides the same not-yet-registered ESS interop fixtures row as pki.schema.smime.parseSigningCertificate. Graduate alongside it once the OpenSSL round-trip lands.

pki.schema.smime.parseSmimeCapabilities.reason: The RFC 8551 SMIMECapabilities decoder's return shape is part of the S/MIME ESS attribute cohort that is not yet independently interop-verified (no OpenSSL/NSS round-trip of the attribute in the harness). Graduate alongside the ESS interop fixtures row.

pki.schema.smime.decodeAttribute.reason: The OID-dispatch entry that routes a CMS attribute to the ESS / SMIMECapabilities decoders; its return shape is frozen only once those decoders are interop-proven. Graduate alongside the ESS interop fixtures row.

pki.est.classifyResponse.reason: The RFC 7030 / 8951 EST client codec is transport-agnostic and not yet interop-proven end-to-end against an EST server oracle in the integration harness (no offline EST server), so its return shape is not frozen. Graduate once an EST round-trip is validated by an independent server.

pki.est.parseCertsOnly.reason: The RFC 7030 / 8951 EST client codec is transport-agnostic and not yet interop-proven end-to-end against an EST server oracle in the integration harness (no offline EST server), so its return shape is not frozen. Graduate once an EST round-trip is validated by an independent server.

pki.est.paths.reason: The RFC 7030 / 8951 EST client codec is transport-agnostic and not yet interop-proven end-to-end against an EST server oracle in the integration harness (no offline EST server), so its return shape is not frozen. Graduate once an EST round-trip is validated by an independent server.

pki.est.transferDecode.reason: The RFC 7030 / 8951 EST client codec is transport-agnostic and not yet interop-proven end-to-end against an EST server oracle in the integration harness (no offline EST server), so its return shape is not frozen. Graduate once an EST round-trip is validated by an independent server.

pki.est.transferEncode.reason: The RFC 7030 / 8951 EST client codec is transport-agnostic and not yet interop-proven end-to-end against an EST server oracle in the integration harness (no offline EST server), so its return shape is not frozen. Graduate once an EST round-trip is validated by an independent server.

pki.acme.assertTransition.reason: The RFC 8555 ACME message layer returns the toolkit's own decoded message shapes (order / account / authorization / challenge objects, problem documents, the state-machine transition verdicts), which are still settling as the transport client surface fills in; graduating would freeze those return shapes. Graduate once the message-layer return shapes are frozen alongside the node:https client.

pki.acme.validateProblem.reason: The RFC 8555 ACME message layer returns the toolkit's own decoded message shapes (order / account / authorization / challenge objects, problem documents, the state-machine transition verdicts), which are still settling as the transport client surface fills in; graduating would freeze those return shapes. Graduate once the message-layer return shapes are frozen alongside the node:https client.

Graduate once the message-layer return shapes are frozen alongside the node:https client.

Graduate once the message-layer return shapes are frozen alongside the node:https client.

Graduate once the message-layer return shapes are frozen alongside the node:https client.

pki.cbor.decode.reason: The deterministic-CBOR read surface awaits its first composing consumer (C509 / WebAuthn / COSE) to prove the reader contract in situ; graduate with that consumer or on a dedicated review.

pki.cbor.read.uint.reason: The deterministic-CBOR read surface awaits its first composing consumer (C509 / WebAuthn / COSE) to prove the reader contract in situ; graduate with that consumer or on a dedicated review.

pki.cbor.read.nint.reason: The deterministic-CBOR read surface awaits its first composing consumer (C509 / WebAuthn / COSE) to prove the reader contract in situ; graduate with that consumer or on a dedicated review.

pki.cbor.read.int.reason: The deterministic-CBOR read surface awaits its first composing consumer (C509 / WebAuthn / COSE) to prove the reader contract in situ; graduate with that consumer or on a dedicated review.

pki.cbor.read.byteString.reason: The deterministic-CBOR read surface awaits its first composing consumer (C509 / WebAuthn / COSE) to prove the reader contract in situ; graduate with that consumer or on a dedicated review.

pki.cbor.read.textString.reason: The deterministic-CBOR read surface awaits its first composing consumer (C509 / WebAuthn / COSE) to prove the reader contract in situ; graduate with that consumer or on a dedicated review.

pki.cbor.read.array.reason: The deterministic-CBOR read surface awaits its first composing consumer (C509 / WebAuthn / COSE) to prove the reader contract in situ; graduate with that consumer or on a dedicated review.

pki.cbor.read.map.reason: The deterministic-CBOR read surface awaits its first composing consumer (C509 / WebAuthn / COSE) to prove the reader contract in situ; graduate with that consumer or on a dedicated review.

pki.cbor.read.boolean.reason: The deterministic-CBOR read surface awaits its first composing consumer (C509 / WebAuthn / COSE) to prove the reader contract in situ; graduate with that consumer or on a dedicated review.

pki.cbor.read.nullValue.reason: The deterministic-CBOR read surface awaits its first composing consumer (C509 / WebAuthn / COSE) to prove the reader contract in situ; graduate with that consumer or on a dedicated review.

pki.cbor.read.undefinedValue.reason: The deterministic-CBOR read surface awaits its first composing consumer (C509 / WebAuthn / COSE) to prove the reader contract in situ; graduate with that consumer or on a dedicated review.

pki.cbor.read.float.reason: The deterministic-CBOR read surface awaits its first composing consumer (C509 / WebAuthn / COSE) to prove the reader contract in situ; graduate with that consumer or on a dedicated review.

pki.cbor.read.biguint.reason: The deterministic-CBOR read surface awaits its first composing consumer (C509 / WebAuthn / COSE) to prove the reader contract in situ; graduate with that consumer or on a dedicated review.

pki.cbor.read.time.reason: The deterministic-CBOR read surface awaits its first composing consumer (C509 / WebAuthn / COSE) to prove the reader contract in situ; graduate with that consumer or on a dedicated review.

pki.cbor.read.oid.reason: The deterministic-CBOR read surface awaits its first composing consumer (C509 / WebAuthn / COSE) to prove the reader contract in situ; graduate with that consumer or on a dedicated review.

pki.merkle.leafHash.reason: Awaiting a dedicated stabilization review with an external interop oracle before the surface freezes.

pki.merkle.nodeHash.reason: Awaiting a dedicated stabilization review with an external interop oracle before the surface freezes.

pki.merkle.emptyRootHash.reason: Awaiting a dedicated stabilization review with an external interop oracle before the surface freezes.

pki.merkle.verifyInclusion.reason: Awaiting a dedicated stabilization review with an external interop oracle before the surface freezes.

pki.merkle.verifyConsistency.reason: Awaiting a dedicated stabilization review with an external interop oracle before the surface freezes.

pki.path.ocspChecker.reason: Awaiting a dedicated stabilization review with an external interop oracle before the surface freezes.

pki.trust.parseCertdata.reason: The NSS certdata.txt trust-store parser's return shape (per-anchor trust bits + distrust metadata) is still absorbing fields as new root-program constraints are modeled; it has no external consumer pinning the object shape. Graduate once the anchor metadata surface is settled and cross-checked against an independent trust-store reader.

pki.trust.parseCcadbCsv.reason: The CCADB CSV trust-store parser shares the anchor metadata surface with pki.trust.parseCertdata and the same not-yet-frozen return shape. Graduate alongside it.

pki.trust.anchor.reason: Constructs the trust anchor that pki.path.validate consumes; its constraint surface tracks the still-experimental pki.path.validate. Graduate alongside pki.path.validate once the path-validation surface has stabilized and is interop-proven.

pki.shbs.verify.reason: The RFC 8554 LMS/HSS verify surface is standard-settled, but it is not yet interop-proven end-to-end: no HSS certificate fixture drives an accept/reject through pki.path.validate's auto-verify row, so the algorithm-identifier dispatch it would expose is not frozen. Graduate once an HSS signature fixture cross-checks against an independent LMS verifier in the integration harness.

pki.shbs.verifyLms.reason: The single-tree LMS verify entry point backs pki.shbs.verify's HSS layer and shares its not-yet-interop-proven status. Graduate alongside pki.shbs.verify once an independent LMS verifier cross-checks a signature it accepts.

pki.hpke.setupS.reason: The RFC 9180 base HPKE construction is standard-settled and proven against the Appendix A known-answer vectors, but the KEM registry is deliberately shaped to admit the ML-KEM and X-Wing PQ-hybrid KEMs as data-row additions once their HPKE bindings stabilize, which may extend the sender-context surface. Graduate once the post-quantum KEM set is settled.

pki.hpke.setupR.reason: Rides the same RFC 9180 KEM registry as pki.hpke.setupS and the same pending post-quantum KEM additions. Graduate alongside pki.hpke.setupS.

pki.hpke.seal.reason: The single-shot seal over an HPKE sender context; its surface tracks the still-experimental pki.hpke.setupS KEM registry. Graduate alongside pki.hpke.setupS once the post-quantum KEM set is settled.

pki.hpke.open.reason: The single-shot open over an HPKE recipient context; its surface tracks the still-experimental pki.hpke.setupR KEM registry. Graduate alongside pki.hpke.setupR.

pki.sigstore.verifyBundle.reason: The offline Sigstore-bundle verifier is proven against a real npm-provenance bundle, but the Sigstore bundle media type and the trusted-root format are still versioning upstream, so the accepted bundle shape is not yet frozen. Graduate once the bundle format version is settled and a second independent bundle source is cross-checked.

pki.sigstore.parseBundle.reason: The bundle reader that backs pki.sigstore.verifyBundle; its return shape tracks the still-versioning Sigstore bundle media type. Graduate alongside pki.sigstore.verifyBundle.

pki.sigstore.pae.reason: The DSSE pre-authentication-encoding helper exposed for the Sigstore verify surface; it graduates with the rest of pki.sigstore once the bundle format is frozen.

pki.inspect.certificate.reason: The rendered report is intentionally OpenSSL-familiar rather than a byte-frozen format (OpenSSL releases disagree), so the output contract is not yet pinned, and the inspection surface is still growing (CRL / CSR / CMS inspection is Targeted). Graduate once the full inspect surface lands and the rendered-output contract is frozen.

pki.lint.certificate.reason: The finding-id surface is frozen in the api-snapshot, but the rule COVERAGE is a representative v1 subset (the full CABF TLS corpus, the S/MIME BR profile, and the CRL / OCSP profiles are Targeted). Graduate once the rule corpus is broadened and cross-checked against a reference linter (zlint / pkilint).

pki.lint.rules.reason: Registry introspection for pki.lint.certificate; its shape tracks the still-expanding rule set. Graduate alongside pki.lint.certificate.

pki.lint.profiles.reason: Enumerates the lint profile names; the profile set will grow as the CABF / S/MIME / CRL / OCSP corpora land. Graduate alongside pki.lint.certificate.

pki.cms.verify.reason: The SignedData verify surface is interop-proven for the classical signers, but the post-quantum acceptance rules (ML-DSA RFC 9882, SLH-DSA RFC 9814, composite ML-DSA) landed across the last three releases and the composite draft is still pre-RFC. Graduate together with pki.cms.sign once the PQC/composite signer set has seasoned a full review cycle and the composite spec reaches RFC.

pki.cms.sign.reason: The CMS SignedData signing surface tracks pki.cms.verify: the post-quantum ML-DSA / SLH-DSA / composite ML-DSA signer arms landed across the last three releases and the composite draft is still pre-RFC. Graduate together with pki.cms.verify once the PQC/composite signer set has seasoned a full review cycle and the composite specification reaches RFC.

pki.tsp.sign.reason: The RFC 3161 timestamp-token producing surface composes pki.cms.sign; it graduates with the CMS signing surface it builds on, once that set has seasoned and the composite signer specification reaches RFC.

pki.schema.tsp.parseRequest.reason: The RFC 3161 timestamp surface is interop-proven against openssl ts, but it composes the still-experimental pki.cms.sign / pki.cms.verify whose composite ML-DSA signer set is pre-RFC (draft-ietf-lamps-cms-composite-sigs); graduate alongside the CMS signing surface once that set is settled.

pki.tsp.request.reason: The RFC 3161 timestamp surface is interop-proven against openssl ts, but it composes the still-experimental pki.cms.sign / pki.cms.verify whose composite ML-DSA signer set is pre-RFC (draft-ietf-lamps-cms-composite-sigs); graduate alongside the CMS signing surface once that set is settled.

pki.tsp.parseRequest.reason: The RFC 3161 timestamp surface is interop-proven against openssl ts, but it composes the still-experimental pki.cms.sign / pki.cms.verify whose composite ML-DSA signer set is pre-RFC (draft-ietf-lamps-cms-composite-sigs); graduate alongside the CMS signing surface once that set is settled.

pki.tsp.response.reason: The RFC 3161 timestamp surface is interop-proven against openssl ts, but it composes the still-experimental pki.cms.sign / pki.cms.verify whose composite ML-DSA signer set is pre-RFC (draft-ietf-lamps-cms-composite-sigs); graduate alongside the CMS signing surface once that set is settled.

pki.tsp.parseResponse.reason: The RFC 3161 timestamp surface is interop-proven against openssl ts, but it composes the still-experimental pki.cms.sign / pki.cms.verify whose composite ML-DSA signer set is pre-RFC (draft-ietf-lamps-cms-composite-sigs); graduate alongside the CMS signing surface once that set is settled.

pki.tsp.verify.reason: The RFC 3161 timestamp surface is interop-proven against openssl ts, but it composes the still-experimental pki.cms.sign / pki.cms.verify whose composite ML-DSA signer set is pre-RFC (draft-ietf-lamps-cms-composite-sigs); graduate alongside the CMS signing surface once that set is settled.

pki.cbor.read.mapGet.reason: The deterministic-CBOR read surface (pki.cbor) is still settling as its COSE / CWT / EAT / C509 consumers land; read.mapGet is a stable map-lookup leaf but its home namespace has no externally frozen consumer yet. Graduate alongside the pki.cbor read surface once the C509 profile and the COSE key/attestation consumers pin it.

pki.ocsp.buildRequest.reason: The RFC 6960 OCSP producer + relying-party surface is interop-proven against openssl ocsp, but it composes the still-experimental pki.cms.sign / pki.cms.verify whose composite ML-DSA signer set is pre-RFC (draft-ietf-lamps-cms-composite-sigs); graduate alongside the CMS signing surface once that set is settled.

pki.ocsp.sign.reason: The RFC 6960 OCSP producer + relying-party surface is interop-proven against openssl ocsp, but it composes the still-experimental pki.cms.sign / pki.cms.verify whose composite ML-DSA signer set is pre-RFC (draft-ietf-lamps-cms-composite-sigs); graduate alongside the CMS signing surface once that set is settled.

pki.ocsp.buildErrorResponse.reason: The RFC 6960 OCSP producer + relying-party surface is interop-proven against openssl ocsp, but it composes the still-experimental pki.cms.sign / pki.cms.verify whose composite ML-DSA signer set is pre-RFC (draft-ietf-lamps-cms-composite-sigs); graduate alongside the CMS signing surface once that set is settled.

pki.ocsp.verify.reason: The RFC 6960 OCSP producer + relying-party surface is interop-proven against openssl ocsp, but it composes the still-experimental pki.cms.sign / pki.cms.verify whose composite ML-DSA signer set is pre-RFC (draft-ietf-lamps-cms-composite-sigs); graduate alongside the CMS signing surface once that set is settled.

pki.path.verifyOcspResponse.reason: The lower-level RFC 6960 relying-party OCSP verify shares the path-validate verify bridge and the same not-yet-interop-proven-against-an-independent-validator revocation path as pki.path.validate; graduate alongside pki.path.validate.

pki.cms.encrypt.reason: The CMS content-encryption surface (all five RFC 5652 recipient arms) is oracle-free and interop-proven against openssl cms, but its post-quantum ML-KEM KEMRecipientInfo arm rides recently-published RFCs (9629/9936) and the composite-KEM draft is unlanded; graduate once the enveloped surface's PQC recipient set is settled.

pki.cms.decrypt.reason: The CMS content-decryption surface (all five recipient arms, oracle-free) is interop-proven against openssl cms, but its post-quantum ML-KEM KEMRecipientInfo arm rides recently-published RFCs (9629/9936) and the composite-KEM draft is unlanded; graduate alongside pki.cms.encrypt once the enveloped surface's PQC recipient set is settled.

pki.smime.encrypt.reason: The RFC 8551 S/MIME encryption layer is bidirectionally interop-proven against openssl cms, but it is a thin frame over pki.cms.encrypt, whose post-quantum ML-KEM recipient arm rides recently-published specs and is itself kept experimental; graduate S/MIME encryption together with the CMS content-encryption surface it composes.

pki.smime.decrypt.reason: The RFC 8551 S/MIME encryption layer is bidirectionally interop-proven against openssl cms, but it is a thin frame over pki.cms.encrypt, whose post-quantum ML-KEM recipient arm rides recently-published specs and is itself kept experimental; graduate S/MIME encryption together with the CMS content-encryption surface it composes.

pki.cms.compress.reason: The RFC 3274 CMS CompressedData surface (ZLIB-only, decompression-bomb bounded) is interop-consistent, but it composes the still-experimental CMS content-type surface; graduate together with the CMS envelope/compress family.

pki.cms.decompress.reason: The RFC 3274 CMS CompressedData surface (ZLIB-only, decompression-bomb bounded) is interop-consistent, but it composes the still-experimental CMS content-type surface; graduate together with the CMS envelope/compress family.

pki.smime.compress.reason: The RFC 8551 S/MIME compressed-data frame is a thin layer over pki.cms.compress, which is itself kept experimental; graduate together with the CMS compression surface it composes.

pki.smime.decompress.reason: The RFC 8551 S/MIME compressed-data frame is a thin layer over pki.cms.decompress, which is itself kept experimental; graduate together with the CMS compression surface it composes.

pki.attrcert.sign.reason: RFC 5755 attribute-certificate issuance is interop-proven (openssl), but its builder opts (the attribute value shapes, the holder and issuer forms, the extension set) are still settling as the attribute-certificate consuming and verifying surface fills in; graduating would freeze those input shapes prematurely. Graduate once the attribute-certificate surface is complete and the builder opts are frozen.

pki.crmf.build.reason: RFC 4211 CRMF CertReqMessages issuance is composed into the CMP enrollment surface, but its builder opts (the CertTemplate field set, the proof-of-possession forms, the registration controls and regInfo) are still settling as the CMP request/response surface fills in; graduating would freeze those input shapes prematurely. Graduate once the CMP enrollment surface is complete and the builder opts are frozen.

pki.cmp.build.reason: RFC 9810 CMP PKIMessage building assembles the full request + CA/responder message surface, but the surrounding CMP capabilities (HTTP transfer RFC 9811, the lightweight-profile RFC 9483 validation rules, incoming-message MAC/signature verification) are still Planned, and the builder's message/opts shapes continue to absorb refinements as they land; graduating would freeze those input shapes prematurely. Graduate once the CMP transfer + verification surface is complete.

pki.crmf.buildCertTemplate.reason: The thin CertTemplate encoder export rides the same still-settling RFC 4211 CertTemplate field set as pki.crmf.build; graduate alongside pki.crmf.build once the CMP enrollment surface is complete and the builder opts are frozen.
